† SMD ≥0.20 indicates a nonnegligible difference in variable distributions between case-patients and control patients. When calculating SMDs for differences in characteristics across multiple strata, the SMD was calculated as the weighted average of the absolute value of the SMD for each pairwise comparison between case-patients and control patients.
§ Specified underlying conditions included any of the specified respiratory conditions; cardiac conditions; immune or oncologic disorders; neurologic conditions; metabolic, genetic, or endocrine disorders; or sickle cell disease.
¶ Includes chronic lung disease, bronchopulmonary dysplasia, cystic fibrosis, assistance required for secretion clearance, reactive airway disease, obstructive sleep apnea, tracheomalacia/laryngomalacia, and congenital airway disorders.
** Includes congenital heart disease, history of cardiac repair (surgery or interventional catheterization), pulmonary hypertension, and congestive heart failure.
